idea.title: Tiny Markov Forge
idea.hook: A pocket-sized Markov chain rolls procedural weapon parts each run — pick three from a streaming bag to assemble a build that survives a 5-wave gauntlet.
idea.prototype: Two phases per run. Phase 1 (draft): a sub-1MB Markov chain (over a hand-authored part vocabulary: barrel/grip/magazine/sight/charm) emits 9 parts, one every turn, into a bag. Player picks exactly 3 (skipping costs a 'pass' token, max 2 passes). Phase 2 (gauntlet): 5 turn-based waves of enemies; weapon stats derived from picked parts (DPS=barrel, ammo=mag, accuracy=sight, etc.). No real-time aiming — just stat math + 1 active ability tied to the rarest tag. Score = waves cleared + leftover HP. Daily-seed Markov state.
idea.mutation_of: 62_tiny_markov_dunge (theme adjacency, different verb — dungeon-navigation → weapon-draft + stat-math gauntlet)
idea.why_novel: Second Markov-chain game in the portfolio, but mechanically distinct from 62_tiny_markov_dunge. The dungeon game exposed seed-editing as the verb over a navigation puzzle; this game exposes the chain's bigram stream as a drafting decision over stat-math combat. Same tiny-PCG theme (~80 bytes of rules), different gameplay loop (pick 3 of 9 + 5-wave turn-based gauntlet vs WASD movement + room-painting). The pass-token economy + 5-rarity stat ladder is the novel mechanic.
idea.non_timer_pressure: pick budget (3 of 9) + pass tokens (2 max) + HP across waves — three stacked resource scarcity axes, no timer anywhere
idea.lose_condition: wave damage drops HP to 0 (any wave can be the killing one if your build is weak)

sources.title: Roguelite weapon-draft mechanic
sources.snippet: Draft-style weapon assembly mechanic in procedural roguelites — pick limited slots from a streaming pool.

sources.title: Sub-1MB ML in hypercasual
sources.snippet: Discussion of small generative models / Markov chains baked into web games — whether they produce useful variety per run.

sources.title: Tiny-model PCG one-off
sources.snippet: Bake a sub-1MB model into a single game just to see if a tiny generator produces interesting variety per run. Pick smallest sensible genre. Goal is a data point on whether sub-1MB ML in a hypercasual ships at all. (2026-05-14)

synthesizer_brief: Today's digest curator picked this from Tim's IDEAS.md backlog as a second exploration of the tiny-PCG theme. 62_tiny_markov_dunge already validated dungeon-navigation as one application; this game tests the same Markov-chain budget against a draft + stat-math format. The note in the assignment flagged theme overlap with 62 explicitly — visual identity emphasises streaming part cards + 3-slot weapon rack + horizontal wave HP row to distinguish from the top-down dungeon. Markov chain is hand-coded (~16 bigrams × 4 chars ≈ 64 bytes of rule data, well under 80).

alternatives.title: Ice Fracture Foreman
alternatives.hook: Tap a frozen lake to crack it in branching fissures, isolating fish into a shrinking pond before the cracks reach your hut.
alternatives.why_novel: Corral-via-fracture spatial puzzle — mechanically distinct from existing ice games.

alternatives.title: Shader Saturday
alternatives.hook: Drag the post-process slider chain to match the day's screenshot — wrong order makes your render look like AI slop and judges roast you.
alternatives.why_novel: Lives-based meta-game over shader chain ordering.

alternatives.title: Token Throttle
alternatives.hook: You're a dispatcher routing tasks to cheap or expensive AI agents — pick wrong and your monthly budget burns before quota refills.

alternatives.title: Tier-1 Pitch
alternatives.hook: Drag elements onto your store page — title, hook, icon, screenshot — and watch English-speaking testers either click Wishlist or bounce in disgust.
